# Glossary

A reference guide to robotics and MRJL terminology.

---

# Section 8: Glossary {.unnumbered}

## A

### Autonomous Period
The portion of a match where the robot operates entirely on pre-programmed instructions, with no driver input. In VEX IQ, typically 45 seconds.

### Alliance
A partnership of two teams that compete together in a match against another alliance.

### Actuation
The process of moving or controlling a mechanism. "Actuate the claw" means to close or open it.

### Arm
A robotic arm mechanism used to lift, reach, or manipulate game pieces.

## B

### Backlash
The clearance between meshing gears that causes a small delay between input and output motion. Can cause accuracy issues.

### Battery Level
The amount of charge remaining in the robot's battery. Lower battery = lower motor power = slower robot.

### Belt Drive
A power transmission system using a continuous loop of material (belt) to transfer motion between pulleys.

### Bumper
A touch sensor that detects when pressed against an object.

### Build Log
Documentation of all building activities, changes, and decisions made during robot construction.

## C

### Calibration
The process of setting reference values for sensors (e.g., zeroing a gyroscope).

### C-Channel
A VEX IQ structural piece with a C-shaped cross-section, used for building frames and supports.

### Chain Drive
A power transmission system using a roller chain and sprockets, similar to a bicycle.

### Competition Driver
The team member who operates the robot controller during driver-controlled periods.

### Deadband
A range of controller input values (usually near zero) that are ignored to prevent jittery movement when the joystick is at rest.

### Degrees of Freedom (DOF)
The number of independent movements a mechanism can make. A standard robot arm typically has 2-3 DOF.

### Drivetrain
The system of motors, wheels, and structure that allows the robot to move. Also called "drive base."

### Driver Period
The portion of a match where the robot is controlled by human drivers. In VEX IQ, typically 75 seconds.

## E

### Encoder
A sensor that measures rotation, providing feedback on motor position and speed.

### Endgame
The final portion of the driver period (usually last 20-30 seconds) where robots attempt high-value scoring actions.

### Engineering Notebook
A detailed documentation of the team's design process, building activities, and reflections.

### EtherNet Cable
A cable used for connecting the programming cable to the VEX IQ Brain.

## F

### Field
The playing surface where matches take place. Includes the game elements and scoring zones.

### Flywheel
A spinning wheel used to store kinetic energy for launching game pieces with consistent power.

## G

### Game Design
The rules and objectives for a specific year's competition. Changes annually.

### Game Element
Objects used in the competition game (balls, cubes, rings, etc.).

### Gear Ratio
The relationship between input and output rotation speed/torque in a gear system.

### Gyroscope / Inertial Sensor
A sensor that measures rotation and orientation, used for precise turning and driving straight.

## H

### Handoff
The transition between autonomous and driver control, or between drivers A and B.

### Hook
A hook-shaped mechanism designed to engage with game elements or field features.

## I

### Intake
A mechanism designed to collect game pieces into the robot.

### Iteration
The process of making repeated improvements to a design through cycles of testing and refinement.

## L

### Lift
A mechanism that raises and lowers the robot or its subsystems. Also called an "elevator."

### Linkage
A system of connected bars used to convert rotational motion to linear motion (and vice versa).

## M

### Mechanism
A mechanical system that performs a specific function (intake, arm, drivetrain, etc.).

### Motor
An actuator that converts electrical energy into rotational motion.

### Motor Port
A connection point on the VEX IQ Brain for motors and sensors.

## N

### Notebook
Short for Engineering Notebook.

## O

### Odometry
The use of sensor data (encoder, gyroscope) to track robot position over time.

## P

### PID Control
Proportional-Integral-Derivative control — an algorithm that smooths and stabilizes motor movements based on sensor feedback.

### Pneumatics
Systems that use compressed air to power mechanisms, providing strong linear actuation.

### Pre-Load
Game pieces that are placed on the robot before the match begins.

### Programming Cable
A cable used to transfer code from a computer to the VEX IQ Brain.

### Prototype
An early version of a mechanism or design for testing purposes.

## Q

### Queue / Queuing
The process of lining up for matches. Teams are called to the field in groups (queues).

## R

### Rack and Pinion
A linear motion system using a gear (pinion) engaging with a toothed bar (rack).

### Radio Link
The wireless connection between the VEX IQ Controller and Brain.

### RPM
Revolutions Per Minute — a measure of motor or mechanism speed.

## S

### Scoring Zone
A designated area of the field where game elements earn points when placed correctly.

### Sensor
A device that provides feedback about the robot's environment or internal state.

### Skills
Individual challenges (not full matches) where robots attempt specific tasks for points.

### Smart Port
VEX IQ's motor and sensor connection system with built-in intelligence.

### Spinning
When a motor is activated and rotating. "Is the motor spinning?"

### Sprocket
A toothed wheel used in chain drive systems.

### Stall
When a motor is applying power but cannot move the load, causing it to draw maximum current.

### Subsystem
A functional division of the robot (drivetrain, intake, arm, etc.).

## T

### Tank Treads
Continuous tracks that provide high traction for driving. Also called "tank treads" or "tracks."

### Tank Drive
A control scheme where two sticks independently control left and right sides of the drivetrain.

### Taxonomy
A classification system. In robotics, often refers to categorizing mechanisms by type.

### Team Number
A unique identifier assigned to each registered team.

### Torque
The rotational force produced by a motor. Higher torque = can move heavier loads.

### Training
Learning activities for new team members before they work on competition robots.

### Tuning
The process of adjusting parameters (PID values, speeds, timings) to optimize performance.

## V

### VEX IQ
The robotics platform used in MRJL competitions.

## W

### Wheelbase
The distance between the left and right wheels on a drivetrain, affecting stability and turning.

### Work Session
A scheduled time when the team meets to build, code, and practice.

---

## Common Abbreviations

| Abbreviation | Full Term |
|--------------|-----------|
| 2D | Two-dimensional |
| 3D | Three-dimensional |
| AI | Artificial Intelligence |
| API | Application Programming Interface |
| auto | Autonomous |
| avg | Average |
| Bat | Battery |
| B/C | Build/Coding (meeting format) |
| B/P | Builder/Pit (role designation) |
| CAD | Computer-Aided Design |
| Cntl | Controller |
| Ctrl | Control |
| D/A | Driver A |
| D/B | Driver B |
| deg | Degrees |
| DOF | Degrees of Freedom |
| Dr | Driver |
| drv | Drivetrain |
| env | Endgame |
| FWD | Forward |
| gear | Gearbox |
| gyro | Gyroscope |
| I/O | Input/Output |
| in | Inches |
| int | Intake |
| LED | Light-Emitting Diode |
| LiPo | Lithium Polymer (battery type) |
| mA | Milliamps (electrical current) |
| max | Maximum |
| mech | Mechanism |
| min | Minimum |
| mm | Millimeters |
| mot | Motor |
| MRJL | Mingwan Junior League |
| N/A | Not Applicable |
| Nav | Navigation |
| No. | Number |
| ODE | Ordinary Differential Equation |
| PID | Proportional-Integral-Derivative |
| PPS | Points Per Second |
| prev | Previous |
| pri | Primary |
| PS | Power Supply |
| PWM | Pulse Width Modulation |
| QP | Qualification Points |
| RAM | Random Access Memory |
| rev | Reverse |
| RMS | Root Mean Square |
| RPM | Revolutions Per Minute |
| sec | Seconds |
| sol | Solenoid |
| Spd | Speed |
| SR | Session Record |
| SSD | Solid State Drive |
| TBD | To Be Determined |
| temp | Temperature |
| tol | Tolerance |
| TOT | Tournament |
| TTL | Transistor-Transistor Logic |
| UART | Universal Asynchronous Receiver-Transmitter |
| USB | Universal Serial Bus |
| V | Volts |
| var | Variable |
| VEX | Vector Electronics |
| VRC | VEX Robotics Competition |
| WD | When Drafted |
| WP | Win Points |

---

::: {.callout-note}
## Contributing to This Glossary

If you encounter terms not defined here or find existing definitions unclear, report them to your team captain for review and update.
:::
